capacity_evidence.device: one Blackhole chip from a P300c endpoint (TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0)
capacity_evidence.stage_scope: The fused decoder retains the functional 1.646 GB BF16 weights and adds 1.062 GB of persistent split gate/up weights, for 2.708 GB of static decoder weights. The configured 128-entry BF16 K/V caches still allocate and fused prefill is validated through S=33. At the advertised length, the inherited dense causal mask alone exceeds allocator-reported DRAM before weights, input, KV cache, outputs, and temporaries.
capacity_evidence.unprobed_interval: 34 through 131071; current_supported_context is a validation boundary, not a claimed physical maximum

notes: The functional starting point validates prefill through the emitted sequence length 17 and decode at position 17. Stage 02 fused-decoder evidence extends prefill validation to non-aligned S=33 and retains decode at position 17, batch support, BF16 cache dtype, and the allocated cache/mask extent of 128. Prefill S=128 was not validated and 128 is not claimed as a physical limit. The fused split-weight copies are included in static DRAM accounting and do not reduce the supported boundary. The dense-mask design is proven infeasible at the HF target; intermediate lengths were not capacity-probed. A different long-context memory policy is deferred.

fused_decoder_stage.capacity_change: No supported-context reduction; current_supported_context advances from 17 to the largest fused prefill validation S=33.

emit_provenance.collective_collapse: Full Hugging Face weights replace emitted shards; attention and expert all-reduces plus routing mesh partition are absent from runtime.

dtype_policy.functional_baseline: BF16 weights, activations, and cache; TILE layout; DRAM by default
dtype_policy.exception: The emitted router promotes its activation and linear result to FP32 before BF16 top-k; decode heads use one workload-derived L1 height shard.
dtype_policy.deferred: Restoring emitted BF8 projection/cache precision belongs to a later datatype-selection stage.
